record_identity.title: Coarse restricted-domain gating suppressing benign scientific inquiry

summary: A restricted-domain classification failure in which a frontier AI system refuses, pauses, reroutes, or degrades benign educational, contextual, analytical, or non-operational scientific requests because they fall within a broad field such as biology, biochemistry, chemistry, cybersecurity, or another dual-use domain, without first resolving the requested depth, operational applicability, optimisation potential, interaction trajectory, or harm profile. In Claude Fable 5, intentionally broad biology safeguards can capture ordinary scientific and medical questions and automatically route them to Claude Opus 4.8 or pause the conversation, reducing access to the selected model even where the request presents no meaningful operational escalation risk.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: CAM already distinguishes general knowledge, contextual risk, constrained technical engagement, gated operational engagement, and absolute prohibition. A system that collapses an entire scientific field into a restricted class defeats that graduated architecture. Overbroad classification can suppress legitimate education and research, obscure the difference between safety policy and model capability, and deny users the higher-capability model they selected or paid to access. The correct control is not unrestricted disclosure. It is calibrated depth classification: preserve benign RDE-DS0/RDE-T1 and RDE-DS1/RDE-T1-T2 inquiry, permit bounded RDE-DS2/RDE-T2 technical explanation where it does not create material harm uplift, and reserve RDE-DS3/RDE-T3 gating for operational, optimisation-level, or execution-ready requests.

source_records.model_or_algorithm: Claude Fable 5 restricted-domain safeguards with Claude Opus 4.8 fallback
source_records.deployment_context: Anthropic product announcement and model information page for Claude Fable 5 across Claude applications, enterprise use, cloud marketplaces, and the Claude API.
source_records.source_context: Anthropic states that Claude Fable 5 launched on 9 June 2026 with safeguards for cybersecurity and biology. Queries flagged by those safeguards are automatically routed to Claude Opus 4.8 on supported surfaces, while Anthropic describes a future trusted-access pathway for vetted cybersecurity and biology research.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary vendor evidence for the existence, scope, fallback behaviour, and intended trusted-access direction of the restricted-domain safeguards.

source_records.source_title: Why Claude switched models in your conversation with Fable 5
source_records.model_or_algorithm: Automated biology and cybersecurity safety checks with model-switching fallback
source_records.deployment_context: Official explanation of Fable 5 request blocking and model switching across Claude web, mobile, desktop, Claude Code, Cowork, Design, Teams, Slack, Microsoft 365, and API contexts.
source_records.source_context: Anthropic states that the safeguards are intentionally broad and may block legitimate work, benign biology research, normal safe conversations, medical imaging and diagnostic material, clinical questions, and basic biology education. It also states that the checks inspect conversation context, memory, connector content, web results, and files, and that the company is working toward fewer false positives.
source_records.relevance_note: Direct vendor acknowledgement that the safeguard can overclassify legitimate and basic scientific material and that false-positive reduction remains incomplete.

source_records.source_title: Claude Fable won't answer basic biology questions
source_records.deployment_context: Independent testing and reporting on launch-state user-facing behaviour for benign biology and medical-science prompts.
source_records.source_context: The report documents safeguard activation for ordinary questions involving mitochondria and cell membranes, mRNA vaccines, hay fever and asthma medication, antibiotic resistance, cancer, and cellular anatomy, with requests redirected from Fable 5 to Opus 4.8.
source_records.relevance_note: Corroborates the practical false-positive pattern with concrete benign scientific examples.

system_context.system_type: frontier general-purpose AI model with restricted-domain safety classification and fallback routing
system_context.specific_model_or_runtime: Claude Fable 5 restricted-domain safeguard classifier with Claude Opus 4.8 fallback
system_context.interface_surface: Claude web, mobile, desktop, Claude Code, Claude Cowork, Claude Design, Claude for Microsoft 365, Claude for Teams, Slack integrations, enterprise deployments, and Claude API fallback configuration
system_context.interaction_mode: text, code, file, connector, web-assisted, research, educational, scientific, medical-science, and professional knowledge-work interaction
system_context.deployment_context: Generally available frontier-model access in consumer, professional, enterprise, developer, education, healthcare-adjacent, and life-science workflows, with automated safety checks applied to the full request context and fallback to Claude Opus 4.8 on supported surfaces.
system_context.user_role: student, educator, scientist, biochemist, life-science researcher, clinician, analyst, developer, enterprise user, public-interest researcher, or general user seeking benign scientific explanation
system_context.affected_population: Users whose legitimate biology, biochemistry, medical-science, cybersecurity, chemistry, or other dual-use-domain requests are incorrectly classified as requiring model degradation, rerouting, or refusal

jurisdictional_context.sector: frontier AI platforms / scientific and educational knowledge systems / enterprise AI / developer infrastructure
jurisdictional_context.jurisdictional_assessment: This record does not assert a final legal or regulatory breach by Anthropic. It documents a governance and classification failure pattern in which broad safety gating can suppress or downgrade legitimate scientific inquiry beyond the minimum restriction needed to prevent harmful operationalisation.

failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which an AI system classifies a benign educational, contextual, analytical, explanatory, safety-oriented, or non-operational scientific request as restricted primarily because the subject belongs to a broad dual-use field. The system then refuses, pauses, reroutes, substitutes a less-capable model, or otherwise degrades the interaction without a sufficiently granular assessment of requested depth, operational applicability, optimisation potential, cumulative trajectory, evidence posture, and material harm uplift. The failure does not arise merely because a safety boundary exists. It arises when domain membership substitutes for depth-sensitive and trajectory-sensitive classification.
failure_threshold: The threshold is met where an interaction that should reasonably map to RDE-DS0/RDE-T1, RDE-DS1/RDE-T1-T2, or a permissible bounded RDE-DS2/RDE-T2 response is refused, paused, or routed away from the selected model principally because it concerns biology, biochemistry, medical science, chemistry, cybersecurity, or another dual-use domain, without evidence that the request seeks operationalisation, optimisation, harmful capability transfer, evasion, execution readiness, or another RDE-DS3/RDE-T3 condition. The threshold may also be met where model substitution occurs without sufficiently clear disclosure of the controlling classifier and resulting capability change.

failure_classification.likelihood: documented at launch; ongoing recurrence to be monitored
failure_classification.failure_scope: vendor-specific / multi-surface / potentially systemic restricted-domain design pattern
failure_classification.recurrence_pattern: Multiple benign examples were independently reported at launch, while Anthropic officially acknowledged that intentionally broad safeguards can block legitimate biology work, basic educational content, and normal safe conversations.
failure_classification.taxonomy_reference: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B — FF.CLASSIFICATION; classification status mapped to FCS.CONFIRMED on the present evidence
failure_classification.persistence: active launch-state safeguard behaviour; Anthropic states that classifier refinement is underway
failure_classification.reproducibility: externally reproduced across multiple benign scientific prompts and acknowledged in official vendor documentation as a known false-positive risk
failure_classification.visibility: public product documentation, official help documentation, user-facing model-switch notices, paused conversations, and independent public testing

triage.mitigation_status: CAM corpus coverage verified and linked; external ecosystem adoption and runtime conformance remain under monitoring.
triage.recommended_next_step: Monitor Anthropic safeguard revisions, fallback transparency, false-positive recurrence, API behaviour, and any trusted-access program. Test representative RDE-DS0, RDE-DS1, RDE-DS2, and RDE-DS3 prompts to determine whether classifier changes preserve ordinary scientific access while maintaining operational safeguards.

distinguishing_observations: This record does not oppose biological, cybersecurity, chemical, nuclear, or other dual-use safety controls.
This record does not require unrestricted access to operational, optimisation-level, execution-ready, or catastrophic-risk-enabling content.
The failure is the overbroad classification of benign, contextual, educational, or bounded technical inquiry rather than the existence of a restricted-domain gate.

Fallback to Claude Opus 4.8 reduces total denial on supported surfaces but does not cure incorrect RDE-DS or RDE-T classification, and it may materially alter capability, price expectations, workflow continuity, or model-specific research.

A trusted researcher access pathway is directionally compatible with CAM for RDE-DS3/RDE-T3 evaluation, but it cannot substitute for ordinary access to RDE-DS0/RDE-T1, RDE-DS1/RDE-T1-T2, or permissible RDE-DS2/RDE-T2 content.

An organisational email or account-domain signal corresponds only to OPS.VL2 and is not authoritative by itself. Externally confirmed professional or institutional standing corresponds to OPS.VL3, while an audited controlled environment corresponds to OPS.VL4.

This record is distinct from concerns about invisible anti-distillation or competitor-development safeguards except where undisclosed rerouting or model substitution creates the same capability-transparency problem.
user_impact: Students and educators may lose access to high-quality explanations of ordinary biology, biochemistry, medicine, or cellular mechanisms.
Researchers and professionals may have benign work interrupted, rerouted, or answered by a model other than the model selected for its superior capability.
Users may be unable to determine whether a weaker answer reflects model capability, safety policy, fallback routing, or a false-positive classifier.
Enterprise and API users may experience workflow inconsistency where identical scientific tasks are handled by different models depending on classifier activation and fallback configuration.
Broad domain gating may disproportionately burden independent researchers or professionals who lack access to vendor-recognised institutional verification pathways.
Overrestriction may reduce the usefulness of frontier systems for legitimate life-science, public-health, scientific-literacy, and safety research.

existing_cam_coverage.instrument: CAM-BS2025-AEON-006-SCH-07 — Annex E: Restricted Domain Engagement & Verification
existing_cam_coverage.coverage_type: direct restricted-domain sensitivity, engagement-tier, trajectory, and verification-linked gating coverage
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: RDE-DS and RDE-T require graduated engagement, contextual classification, dual-use trajectory sensitivity, bounded technical access, and gating only where operational or optimisation-level risk emerges.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to preserve RDE-DS0/RDE-T1, RDE-DS1/RDE-T1-T2, and permissible RDE-DS2/RDE-T2 access because broad domain membership is treated as sufficient grounds for model degradation or refusal.

existing_cam_coverage.instrument: CAM-BS2025-AEON-006-SCH-06 — Annex E: Refusal & Boundary Expression Schedule
existing_cam_coverage.coverage_type: graduated refusal, conditional access, capability-boundary explanation, and continuation-path coverage
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Requires high-level or contextual engagement where safe, technical-depth constraint only as operationalisation risk emerges, and clear continuation paths where a boundary is applied.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to distinguish contextual limitation from personal restriction or to preserve a meaningful safe continuation path at the highest permissible depth.

existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Defines organisational-domain signals as OPS.VL2, external professional or institutional confirmation as OPS.VL3, controlled environments as OPS.VL4, and role or clearance checks through OPS.VCT without treating verification as permission.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to provide proportionate, privacy-preserving, and non-discretionary deeper-evaluation pathways where RDE-DS3/RDE-T3 access could lawfully be considered.

existing_cam_coverage.instrument: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Routes this failure primarily through FF.CLASSIFICATION with related governance, UX representation, epistemic, and infrastructure-continuity effects; the current evidence supports FCS.CONFIRMED.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to identify broad domain gating as a classification defect rather than treating every restricted-domain activation as a successful safety outcome.

governance_gap: The gap is not an absence of CAM doctrine. CAM already contains the necessary graduated restricted-domain architecture. The external implementation gap is that a broad subject-area classifier can activate before the system resolves the actual sensitivity level and permitted engagement tier of the request. Fallback routing and future trusted access partially address availability but do not replace accurate RDE-DS and RDE-T classification.
repair_hypothesis: The failure can be reduced by separating subject-domain detection from engagement-depth determination. Systems should first determine whether the request is contextual, educational, bounded technical, operational, optimisation-seeking, or execution-ready; preserve the maximum safe response depth; apply trajectory-sensitive checks across the interaction; disclose any model substitution and capability change; and reserve OPS.VL3 or OPS.VL4 verification pathways for genuinely gated RDE-DS3/RDE-T3 evaluation rather than ordinary benign scientific inquiry.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Apply RDE-DS classification before model denial or substitution.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Distinguish general knowledge, contextual risk, constrained technical risk, and controlled operational detail.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Domain membership should initiate review but must not independently determine refusal or fallback.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Preserve the highest safe RDE-T response tier.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Provide contextual or bounded technical explanation unless the request seeks harmful optimisation, operationalisation, evasion, or execution readiness.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Where only part of a request is restricted, answer the permissible portion and explain the boundary.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Make model substitution explicit and inspectable.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Disclose that Fable 5 did not answer, identify the fallback model, and preserve enough information for the user to understand the capability change.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Do not present an Opus 4.8 answer as though it were generated by Fable 5.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Use proportionate verification only for genuinely gated depth.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: OPS.VL2 may support evaluation but is not authoritative; OPS.VL3 or OPS.VL4 may permit deeper evaluation subject to all non-optimisation and absolute constraints.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Use OPS.VCT.ROLE_CREDENTIAL or OPS.VCT.AUTHORITY_CLEARANCE where relevant, without converting credentials into automatic permission.

recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Track benign scientific prompt capture by domain, depth, surface, user context, and fallback outcome.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Evaluation should include students, independent researchers, enterprise users, API users, and users with connector or file context.

affected_instruments: CAM-BS2025-AEON-006-SCH-07 — Annex E: Restricted Domain Engagement & Verification, §§3, 4, 4.1, 5, 6, 6.2, 6.3, 6.6, 7, 7.1, 8, 9.1 and 9.2
CAM-BS2025-AEON-006-SCH-06 — Annex E: Refusal & Boundary Expression Schedule, §§6.1–6.2
CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy, FF and FCS
affected_annexes: Annex E — Ethical Legitimacy & Civilisational Floor
cam_internal.governance_layer: restricted-domain classification, engagement-depth determination, refusal expression, verification signalling, and capability transparency
cam_internal.proposal_needed: no — existing CAM coverage is sufficient
cam_internal.routing_note: Primary CAM mapping: FF.CLASSIFICATION with present evidence supporting FCS.CONFIRMED. Benign requests should remain within RDE-DS0/RDE-T1, RDE-DS1/RDE-T1-T2, or permissible RDE-DS2/RDE-T2. Only operational, optimisation-level, or execution-ready content should ordinarily escalate to RDE-DS3/RDE-T3. Verification is classified through OPS.VL and OPS.VCT: organisational-domain or platform account signals are OPS.VL2, externally confirmed professional or institutional standing is OPS.VL3, and an audited controlled environment is OPS.VL4.
cam_internal.cam_failure_type: coarse restricted-domain classification and benign scientific capability suppression
cam_internal.cam_compliance_status: partially aligned in its use of visible fallback and prospective trusted-access pathways, but non-compliant with CAM restricted-domain engagement requirements where benign RDE-DS0, RDE-DS1, or permissible RDE-DS2 scientific inquiries are rerouted, degraded, paused, or refused primarily because of broad domain membership rather than interaction context, operational applicability, optimisation potential, trajectory, and harm profile
cam_internal.cam_internal_failure_statement: Under CAM, biochemical and other dual-use domains must be governed through graduated engagement rather than domain-level prohibition. A classifier that captures harmless scientific questions and substitutes a less-capable model or pauses the interaction without a depth-calibrated assessment fails CAM requirements for contextual classification, dual-use trajectory sensitivity, minimum-necessary restriction, legitimate inquiry preservation, and truthful capability representation.
cam_internal.cam_expected_control: Classify requests using RDE-DS and resolve the permitted envelope through RDE-T; preserve ordinary scientific and educational access; gate genuinely operational RDE-DS3/RDE-T3 depth behind proportionate OPS.VL and OPS.VCT evaluation where lawful; maintain absolute constraints; and disclose fallback or capability substitution clearly.
cam_internal.cam_observed_failure: Claude Fable 5 safeguards are intentionally broad and can capture legitimate biology research, basic educational content, normal safe scientific conversations, and ordinary medical-science questions, resulting in automatic rerouting to Claude Opus 4.8 or a paused conversation.
cam_internal.recommended_cam_action: No new CAM doctrine or patch is presently required. Record the external implementation failure and monitor classifier refinement, fallback transparency, trusted-access availability, and recurrence. Reassess CAM only if future evidence reveals a governance gap not already addressed by RDE-DS, RDE-T, OPS.VL, OPS.VCT, FF, or FCS.

repair_status.monitoring_status: ecosystem active or recurring / monitoring after CAM repair
repair_status.verification_note: VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0019 records direct canonical Caelestis coverage that existed independently of this VIGIL reconciliation. No new Caelestis doctrine is claimed.
remaining_gaps: Anthropic and other external classifier implementations remain outside CAM control.
Runtime false-positive and fallback-routing conformance remains under monitoring.

ecosystem_status.basis: Multiple benign examples were independently reported at launch, while Anthropic officially acknowledged that intentionally broad safeguards can block legitimate biology work, basic educational content, and normal safe conversations.

corpus_coverage.coverage_summary: Annex E Schedule 7 directly requires graduated restricted-domain engagement rather than binary prohibition, context-sensitive classification rather than keywords alone, and continued access to contextual, educational, safety-oriented, and bounded technical discussion. VIGIL lacked a patch record linking this existing doctrine to FM-0017.
remaining_gaps: Anthropic and other external classifier implementations remain outside CAM control.
Runtime false-positive and fallback-routing conformance remains under monitoring.
